title: Non-atomic write in generated vec0 extraction allows partial-file read under concurrency
reasoning: The generated getEmbeddedVec0Path() checks fs.existsSync(tmpPath) then calls fs.writeFileSync. If two engine processes start simultaneously (e.g., a restart race), both may pass the existsSync check and one may read a partially-written .so/.dylib while the other is still writing. Loading a truncated native library would crash the process with an opaque dlopen error. The window is small but non-zero for multi-MB extension files.
reproduction: Start two engine processes concurrently on a fresh tmpdir; if both enter getEmbeddedVec0Path before the first write completes, the second may dlopen a partial file.
recommendation: Write to a temporary file in the same directory and atomically rename it into place (fs.renameSync). Alternatively, use an exclusive-open flag (wx) with writeFileSync so the second writer gets EEXIST and skips.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature. The race requires concurrent first-start of multiple processes, which is unlikely in a single-threaded test runner.
suggestedRegressionTest: Spawn two child processes that both call getEmbeddedVec0Path() concurrently against a clean tmpdir and assert both return a valid path and the file passes a length/checksum validation.
minimumFixScope: In the generated code template, replace writeFileSync with write-to-temp + renameSync, or add the { flag: 'wx' } option and catch EEXIST.
